title: Preparation of 3.7% Paraformaldehyde Solution
author: Institute of Medical Biotechnology
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcfpitmn

steps.title: Prepare DPBS and Equipment
steps.instruction: For 1 L, 3.7% Formaldehyde, add 800 mL, 1X DPBS to a glass beaker on a stir plate. Be sure to work under a ventilated fume hood.

steps.title: Add Paraformaldehyde
steps.instruction: Add 37 g, paraformaldehyde powder to the heated DPBS solution.

steps.title: Dissolve PFA and Adjust pH (Optional)
steps.instruction: Keep stirring until all PFA powder is dissolved. Take care that the solution does not boil to prevent formic acid formation. For better dissolubility of PFA, you can add NaOH -- one drop at the time.

steps.title: Adjust pH
steps.instruction: Once all PFA powder is dissolved, adjust the pH to pH 7.4 using HCl.

steps.title: Adjust Volume
steps.instruction: Add DPBS subsequently until the total volume of 1 L is reached.

steps.title: Store Solution
steps.instruction: Transfer the solution into tubes of the desired proportions and store at -20 °C until use.
